name: Chuck Conways O'breads Rye Currant Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the starter and water.
2. Mix in half of the white flour and half of the rye flour, one cup at a time, and salt.
3. Stir in the currants, remaining rye flour, and enough white flour to make a stiff dough.
4. When the dough is too stiff to stir, turn it out onto a lightly floured work surface.
5. Knead until it comes together into a mass; it will still be slightly sticky (about 8 minutes).
6. Transfer the dough to a clean mixing bowl and cover. Set aside until doubled in size.
7. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and gently knead for a few seconds.
8. Divide the dough in half and roll each half into a log about 3 inches in diameter.
9. Cut each log into 12 rolls.
10. Roll each piece of dough into a small, tight round using your palm.
11. Place the rolls about 2 inches apart on a cornmeal-dusted baking sheet.
12. Cover and set aside to rise until barely doubled.
13. Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C).
14. For crusty rolls, spray the walls of the oven with water from a plant mister.
